market.advice: Market remains healthy: SPY above its 50-day, breadth at 66% of stocks above their 50-day MA, VIX calm at 16.6 and falling, only 1 distribution day on the tape. Leadership is broadening away from mega-cap tech (NVDA, MSFT, ORCL, PLTR, SMCI all screened WEAK/WATCH here) into financials, industrials, healthcare and utilities ahead of bank earnings season — a healthy rotation, not a warning sign. Geopolitical headlines (Iran/Israel, Khamenei funeral coverage) are pressuring oil lower but haven't dented equity breadth. Stay long with normal position sizing; be selective in mega-cap tech and don't chase names already extended (RSI > 72) into resistance.

picks.name: The Bank of New York Mellon
picks.thesis: Highest-quality setup in the whole scan: a textbook 2-contraction VCP tightening to 3% with the RS line at new highs and EPS +42%/revenue +13% YoY behind it. No independent TV read resolved for BK, but the base is as clean as they come.
strengths: Tightest VCP in the universe (3.0%) with RS at new highs — institutions are leading, not following
EPS +42% / revenue +13% YoY backs the technical strength
risks: Earnings in 13 days — binary event risk into the print
No TradingView cross-check available for this ticker
watch: Trim into strength ahead of the July 15 print

picks.name: JPMorgan Chase
picks.thesis: Money-center bank leadership continues into earnings season — 6-contraction VCP tightened to 2.3% and TradingView's 15-buy/1-sell STRONG_BUY confirms the breakout thesis.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY (15 buy / 1 sell) — full agreement with our signal
EPS +17% / revenue +13% YoY, fwd P/E only 14x
risks: RSI 70 and just 0.3% off the 52-week high — little room before resistance
Earnings in 12 days

picks.name: Citigroup
picks.thesis: Citi's turnaround shows up in the numbers — EPS +56% YoY — and the chart backs it up with a 3-contraction VCP and RS +8.8% over 4 weeks. TV agrees (12 buy / 4 sell, BUY).
strengths: EPS +56% / revenue +16% YoY — best fundamental momentum among the money-centers
Cheapest fwd P/E in the group at 11x
risks: Earnings in 12 days
TV's BUY (not STRONG_BUY) is a notch softer than our score implies

strengths: TV STRONG_BUY with zero sell signals — cleanest cross-check in the batch
EPS +18% / revenue +14% YoY

Earnings in 13 days

picks.name: Aflac

strengths: TV STRONG_BUY (16 buy / 1 sell)
Revenue +28% YoY — real top-line acceleration

Earnings not until 36 days out — less of a near-term catalyst than the banks

EPS +40% / revenue +12% YoY
risks: Fwd P/E 33x — priced for continued execution
TV rates BUY, not STRONG_BUY — modest divergence

picks.name: Valero Energy

strengths: Tight 2.2% VCP at a new high
Cheap 12.5x forward multiple

picks.name: Sirius XM Holdings
picks.thesis: TV is unanimous (16/1/9, STRONG_BUY) and it's at a new high, but RSI 84 is the most overbought reading in this entire list — real tension between a strong technical signal and a stock that's simply run too far, too fast.
strengths: TV STRONG_BUY (16/1/9) — one of the strongest cross-check agreements here
Cheap 8.9x forward multiple
risks: RSI 84 — the most extended reading in this whole batch, high odds of a near-term pause or pullback
Revenue growth only +1% YoY — the move is more technical than fundamental
watch: Wait for RSI to cool below 70 before adding; don't chase at 84

picks.name: SpaceX
picks.thesis: New listing — not yet in our scanned universe (insufficient trading history for VCP/RS/52-week signals), so this is a fundamentals-and-momentum call, not a quant one. Bloomberg reported analysts setting price targets that test a ~$2.2T valuation on its debut as public analyst coverage begins, reflecting Starlink's recurring-revenue scale and Starship/reusability economics.
strengths: Best-in-class reusable-launch cost structure and a large, growing Starlink subscription base give it a real earnings engine beyond one-off launch contracts
Heavy retail and institutional demand around the analyst debut per Bloomberg coverage
risks: TradingView's rating (STRONG_SELL) is based on only 4 total signals — not enough trading history for its indicators to mean anything yet; disregard it
No 52-week chart history — this is a story/valuation trade, not a technical one, and a ~$2.2T valuation leaves very little room for execution missteps
New-listing volatility can be extreme in both directions
watch: This is a new listing — label it as such; re-evaluate once 2-3 months of trading history accumulate real VCP/RS signals
